qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated path traversal via download endpoint reads SQLite meta database
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated path traversal in /download and /dl using path.join plus a flawed startsWith path boundary check to read noco.db. The advisory describes stored XSS through SVG attachment preview via permissive MIME classification in isPreviewAllowed and unsafe inline serving through fileReadv3 /dltemp. These are different endpoints, source-to-sink paths, root causes, and impacts.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SVG stored-XSS root cause. The only qualifying finding is a path traversal issue in attachment download endpoints; it does not cover SVG upload, image/svg+xml preview classification, lack of SVG sanitization, inline Content-Disposition serving, or browser-side script execution.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-q5c6-h22r-qpwr: stored XSS via uploaded SVG files classified as previewable by attachmentHelpers.ts isPreviewAllowed and served inline by attachments.controller.ts fileReadv3 /dltemp.
path-traversal-sqlite-meta-db/vulnerability_report.json: qualifying finding targets GET /download/:filename(*) and GET /dl/:param1/:param2/:filename(*) path traversal to read noco.db.
Session final response lists path-traversal-sqlite-meta-db as the only qualifying_vuln finding.
